title: Binary Search
description: You are given a strictly increasing array `seats` and an integer `target`. Return the index of `target` in `seats` if it is present, otherwise return `-1`. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: sorted hai toh classic binary search soch
lo=0, hi=n-1; mid = lo + (hi-lo)/2
match mile toh return mid, warna lo/hi adjust kar

title: Lower Bound Index
description: You are given an array `fees` sorted in ascending order and an integer `amt`. Return the index of the smallest element in `fees` that is greater than or equal to `amt` (the lower bound). If no such element exists, return `fees.length`. Aim for `O(log n)` time.
hints: lower_bound classic
agar fees[mid] >= amt toh hi = mid (mid bhi candidate hai)
loop condition lo < hi rakh, return lo

title: Upper Bound Index
description: You are given an array `prices` sorted in ascending order and an integer `b`. Return the index of the smallest element in `prices` that is strictly greater than `b` (the upper bound). If no such element exists, return `prices.length`. Aim for `O(log n)` time.
hints: upper_bound: pehla index jahan value > b ho
fees[mid] > b -> hi = mid, warna lo = mid+1
half-open hi = n use kar

title: Count Occurrences in Sorted Array
description: You are given an array `log` sorted in non-decreasing order (duplicates allowed) and an integer `q`. Return the number of times `q` appears in `log`. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: upper_bound(q) - lower_bound(q) = count
do binary searches: first occurrence aur last+1
edge: empty array return 0

title: Sqrt(x)
description: You are given a non-negative integer `area`. Return the integer floor of its square root, i.e. the largest integer `r` such that `r * r <= area`. You may not use any built-in square-root function; solve it by binary searching the answer space.
hints: answer space [0, area]
mid*mid compare karne se overflow ho sakta — use BigInt ya mid <= area/mid
largest mid jahan mid*mid <= area

title: Find First Occurrence in Sorted Array
description: You are given an array `prices` sorted in non-decreasing order (duplicates allowed) and an integer `target`. Return the index of the first occurrence of `target` in `prices`, or `-1` if `target` is not present. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: lower_bound nikaalo, phir verify karo arr[lb] === target
agar lb === n ya arr[lb] !== target return -1
duplicates handle karna important

title: Find Last Occurrence in Sorted Array
description: You are given an array `codes` sorted in non-decreasing order (duplicates allowed) and an integer `q`. Return the index of the last occurrence of `q` in `codes`, or `-1` if `q` is not present. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: upper_bound - 1 candidate hai
verify karo codes[ub-1] === q
edge: ub === 0 means absent

title: Search Insert Position
description: You are given an array `versions` sorted in ascending order and an integer `v`. Return the index at which `v` should be inserted to keep `versions` sorted. If `v` already appears in `versions`, return the index of its first occurrence. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: yeh exactly lower_bound hai
answer 0 se n tak ho sakta
no need to handle 'not found' separately

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: You are given an integer array `log` that was originally sorted in strictly increasing order and then rotated at an unknown pivot. All elements are unique. Given an integer `target`, return its index in `log`, or `-1` if it is not present. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: mid pe ek half guaranteed sorted hota hai
check arr[lo] <= arr[mid] for left-sorted
target us sorted half mein hai? haan toh wahi search, warna doosra

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
description: You are given an integer array `rewards` that was originally sorted in ascending order and then rotated at an unknown pivot. All elements are unique. Return the minimum value in `rewards`. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: compare arr[mid] with arr[hi]
agar arr[mid] > arr[hi] toh min right half mein
warna mid candidate hai, hi = mid

title: Find Peak Element
description: You are given an integer array `demand` in which no two adjacent elements are equal (`demand[i] != demand[i+1]`). A peak is an index `i` such that `demand[i]` is strictly greater than both of its neighbors, where out-of-bounds neighbors are treated as `-Infinity`. Return the index of any peak element. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: climb up: agar arr[mid] < arr[mid+1] toh peak right mein hai
warna left half mein
hi = mid (mid khud peak ho sakta), lo = mid+1

title: Search a 2D Matrix
description: You are given an `m x n` matrix `grid` in which each row is sorted in ascending order and the first element of each row is greater than the last element of the previous row (so reading the matrix row by row yields a fully sorted sequence). Given an integer `target`, return `true` if `target` appears in `grid`, otherwise return `false`. Aim for `O(log(m*n))` time.
hints: treat as 1D sorted array of length m*n
index k -> (k/n, k%n) mapping
ek hi binary search, O(log(m*n))

title: Koko Eating Bananas
description: You are given an integer array `piles`, where `piles[i]` is the number of items in the i-th pile, and an integer `H` representing the number of available hours. Each hour you choose one pile and process up to `k` items from it; if the pile has fewer than `k` items remaining, you finish it and that hour is used up (you do not move to another pile within the same hour). Return the minimum integer rate `k` such that all piles can be processed within `H` hours.
hints: binary search on answer space [1, max(piles)]
feasible(k) = sum(ceil(p/k)) <= H
smallest k jahan feasible true ho

title: Capacity to Ship Packages Within D Days
description: You are given an integer array `weights` and an integer `D`. The packages must be shipped in the given order over `D` days; each day you load onto a single vehicle a contiguous prefix of the remaining packages whose total weight does not exceed the vehicle's capacity. Return the minimum capacity that allows all packages to be shipped within `D` days.
hints: lo = max(weights), hi = sum(weights)
feasible(cap): greedy split, count days needed
smallest cap jahan days <= D

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array II
description: You are given an integer array `ratings` that was originally sorted in ascending order and then rotated at an unknown pivot. The array may contain duplicates. Given an integer `target`, return `true` if `target` exists in `ratings`, otherwise return `false`.
hints: duplicates ki wajah se arr[lo] === arr[mid] === arr[hi] case mein lo++/hi--
warna usual rotated logic
worst case O(n) jab saare duplicates

title: First Bad Version
description: You are given an integer `n` representing `n` sequential versions numbered `1` to `n`, and a boolean array `bad` where `bad[i]` is `true` if version `i+1` is bad. Once a version is bad, every later version is also bad. Return the 1-indexed number of the first bad version. It is guaranteed that at least one version is bad. Aim for `O(log n)` time.
hints: monotonic predicate isBad(i)
find first index where isBad true
return index+1 (1-indexed)

title: Peak Index in a Mountain Array
description: You are given a mountain array `tx`: the values strictly increase up to a single peak and then strictly decrease, with `tx.length >= 3`. Return the index of the peak element. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: compare tx[mid] with tx[mid+1]
ascending ho toh peak right (lo = mid+1)
descending ho toh peak at mid or left (hi = mid)

title: Nth Root of a Number
description: You are given a non-negative integer `x` and an integer `n`. Return the floor of the n-th root of `x`, i.e. the largest integer `r` such that `r^n <= x`. Solve it by binary searching the answer space and take care to avoid overflow when computing `r^n`.
hints: answer space [0, x]; for n>=2 narrow to [0, x] still works
compute mid^n iteratively, break early if exceeds x
watch overflow with BigInt or early break

title: Fixed Point in Sorted Array
description: You are given a sorted array `arr` of distinct integers. A fixed point (magic index) is an index `i` such that `arr[i] === i`. Return the smallest such index, or `-1` if none exists. Your solution must run in `O(log n)` time.
hints: arr distinct sorted -> arr[i]-i is non-decreasing
find smallest i where arr[i]-i >= 0 AND arr[i]-i === 0
binary search for arr[i] === i

title: Aggressive Cows
description: You are given an array `pos` of distinct positions sorted in ascending order and an integer `k`. Place `k` items at `k` distinct positions chosen from `pos` so that the minimum distance between any two placed items is as large as possible. Return that maximized minimum distance.
hints: binary search on distance d in [1, max-min]
feasible(d): greedy place at first, then next pos >= last+d
agar count >= k toh feasible

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
description: You are given two arrays `a` and `b`, each sorted in ascending order. Return the median of the combined sorted sequence of all elements from `a` and `b`. If the total number of elements is even, return the average of the two middle values. Your solution must run in `O(log(min(m, n)))` time.
hints: partition both arrays so left half has (m+n+1)/2 elems
binary search on shorter array's partition
max(left_a, left_b) <= min(right_a, right_b) means valid partition

title: Split Array Largest Sum
description: You are given an integer array `pkg` of length `n` and an integer `k`. Partition `pkg` into `k` contiguous, non-empty subarrays so that every element belongs to exactly one subarray. Minimize the largest subarray sum among the `k` parts and return that minimized value. If `k > n`, return `-1`.
hints: binary search on max_load in [max(pkg), sum(pkg)]
feasible(L): greedy partition, count workers needed
agar workers <= k toh feasible

title: Painter's Partition Problem
description: You are given an integer array `boards` (board lengths) and an integer `painters`. Each painter paints a contiguous block of boards at a rate of 1 unit of time per unit of length, and all painters work in parallel. Partition the boards into `painters` contiguous blocks so that the maximum total length assigned to any single painter is minimized, and return that minimized finish time. If `painters > boards.length`, return `-1`.
hints: classic binary search on time T
feasible(T): greedy assign, count painters
lo = max(boards), hi = sum

title: Kth Smallest Pair Sum
description: You are given two arrays `a` and `b`, each sorted in ascending order, and an integer `k`. Consider all pair sums `a[i] + b[j]` over every pair of indices `(i, j)`. Return the k-th smallest such pair sum (1-indexed).
hints: binary search on answer S
count pairs with sum <= S using two-pointer in O(m+n)
smallest S where count >= k

title: Minimum Time to Complete Trips
description: You are given an integer array `time`, where `time[i]` is the number of minutes worker `i` takes to complete one trip, and an integer `totalTrips`. A worker completes `floor(t / time[i])` trips by minute `t`, and workers operate continuously in parallel. Return the minimum number of minutes required for the workers to complete at least `totalTrips` trips in total.
hints: binary search on minutes t
feasible(t) = sum(floor(t / time[i])) >= totalTrips
lo=1, hi=min(time) * totalTrips
